raw_response:
{
  "communicative_function": "asserting a causal moral-physical principle linking thought quality to bodily outcomes, functioning as prescriptive guidance or inspirational axiom",
  "implicit_claims": [
    "thoughts exert direct, rapid causal control over physiological health and aging",
    "moral valence of thoughts ('unlawful' vs. 'glad and beautiful') determines their somatic effects",
    "disease/decay and youth/beauty are primarily products of mental command rather than external or biological factors"
  ],
  "coherence_assessment": "internally coherent as a symmetrical antithesis but rests on unstated dualistic assumptions without empirical grounding or mechanism",
  "conditions_for_usefulness": "the statement is treated metaphorically to promote psychological well-being practices whose indirect health benefits are already supported by evidence",
  "conditions_for_misleading": "the statement is interpreted literally as medical or causal truth, displacing established factors such as pathogens, genetics, nutrition, and environment"
}
